hypothesis: If metal superhydride discovery is structured around chemical concepts (specifically, identifying metal lattices with strong interstitial electron localization as quasi-atoms prior to hydrogen placement), then combining this chemical template theory with machine learning screening will more efficiently identify thermodynamically stable and superconducting metal superhydrides than unguided high-throughput structural searches, because the chemical framework constrains the search space to physically meaningful configurations that are more likely to yield stable high-hydrogen-content compounds with favorable electronic properties for superconductivity.
